commands.review_summary:
The PR changes WSL setup preflight diagnostics so the virtualization-disabled remediation uses ARM64-specific wording while preserving x64 wording, with focused SetupEngine tests.

Reproducibility: yes. source-level: current main feeds the observed WSL virtualization-disabled text into one helper branch that always returns x86 BIOS/VT-x/AMD-V wording. I did not run a live ARM64 Windows setup session in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] This PR needs contributor-supplied real behavior proof and normal maintainer review; there is no narrow automated repair to queue.

target.title: Use Arm64-aware wording when WSL reports virtualization off
target.body:
The SetupEngine preflight-wsl step previously surfaced x86-only firmware terminology (VT-x/AMD-V, BIOS/UEFI) when wsl.exe reported 'virtualization is not enabled'. On ARM64 devices (e.g. Surface) those terms don't apply, leaving the remediation message confusing.

Split TryGetEnvironmentIssue into a public 1-arg method that delegates to a new internal overload taking System.Runtime.InteropServices. Architecture. The Arm64 branch emits UEFI-/Intune-aware wording; other architectures keep the existing x64 wording. RuntimeInformation. OSArchitecture reflects OS arch (not process arch), so this works even when the app runs as win-x64 under Prism on ARM64 Windows.

Fixes #593
target.author: rbrid
